info.title: Habitat API
info.summary: OpenAPI spec generated from Habitat's AT Protocol lexicons.
info.description: Welcome to the Habitat API reference! We are currently under development and our API may make breaking changes without notice. Stay tuned [@habitat.network](https://bsky.app/profile/habitat.network) on Bluesky and [habitat.leaflet.pub](https://habitat.leaflet.pub) for future formal releases.

get.description: Get this instance's admin-configurable settings. Requires an authenticated instance admin session.

post.description: Issue a single-use invite token for creating an org on this instance. Requires an authenticated instance admin session.

token.description: Signed, single-use invite token to embed in an org-creation link.

post.description: Update this instance's admin-configurable settings. Requires an authenticated instance admin session.

instanceName.description: This instance's display name. Omit to leave unchanged.

post.description: Add member(s) to a clique. The clique must be editable by the caller.
Create a clique. The clique is owned by the caller. Optionally provide members to be added to this clique.

clique.description: The habitat clique, formatted as clique:<owner did>/<clique key>

get.description: See the member(s) to a clique. The clique must be readable by the caller.

parameters.name: clique
parameters.description: The desired clique to query formatted as clique:<owner did>/<clique key>

schema.description: The desired clique to query formatted as clique:<owner did>/<clique key>

get.description: See if a user belongs to a clique. The clique must be readable by the caller.

parameters.name: clique
parameters.description: The desired clique to query formatted as clique:<owner did>/<clique key>

schema.description: The desired clique to query formatted as clique:<owner did>/<clique key>

found.description: Whether the given member is a part of the given clique.

post.description: Remove member(s) to a clique. The clique must be editable by the caller.

get.description: List the record collections (lexicon types) present in the org's synced data, each with a count of the distinct records the calling user can see. Only collections with at least one visible record are returned. Implemented by the home server and reached via pear service proxying.

List the records in a collection the calling user can see, each with the spaces it belongs to that the user can read. The record body is not included; fetch it on demand from pear. Implemented by the home server and reached via pear service proxying.

parameters.name: collection
parameters.description: The NSID of the record collection to list.

schema.description: The NSID of the record collection to list.

post.description: Create a new collaborative document. Implemented by the docs server, which writes the canonical record into the org's docs space using the org credential.

uri.description: URI of the created document record.

docId.description: The record key identifying the document, used in subsequent updateDoc calls.

get.description: List all documents in the org, with titles. Implemented by the docs server, which lists the doc spaces from pear using the org credential and reads each space's markdown 'self' record for the title.

post.description: Apply a CRDT update to a collaborative document. Implemented by the docs server, which merges the update into the canonical document and writes it back using the org credential.

docId.description: The record key identifying the document to update.

update.description: Base64-encoded Yjs update to merge into the document.

uri.description: URI of the updated document record.

post.description: Add a member to a group. The member is either an individual user (subjectDid) or another group whose members are inherited (subjectGroup). The home server writes the backing relationship tuple using the org credential. Caller must be able to manage the group.

group.description: URI of the group-space to add the member to.

subjectDid.description: DID of the user to add as a member. Mutually exclusive with subjectGroup.

subjectGroup.description: URI of another group-space whose members this group should inherit. Mutually exclusive with subjectDid.

uri.description: URI of the written relationship tuple.

post.description: Create a new group. The home server creates a network.habitat.group space using the org credential, writes a network.habitat.group.profile self record, and grants the calling user the manager role so they are both a member and able to manage the group.

Remove a member from a group. The member is either an individual user (subjectDid) or an inherited group (subjectGroup). The home server deletes the backing relationship tuple using the org credential. Caller must be able to manage the group.

group.description: URI of the group-space to remove the member from.

subjectDid.description: DID of the user to remove. Mutually exclusive with subjectGroup.

subjectGroup.description: URI of an inherited group-space to stop inheriting. Mutually exclusive with subjectDid.

get.description: Fetch a single group with its full membership expanded, including which other groups it inherits members from. Implemented by the home server and reached via pear service proxying.

parameters.name: group
parameters.description: URI of the group-space.

schema.description: URI of the group-space.

get.description: List the groups visible to the calling user: groups they are a member of (directly or through inherited groups) and groups they can manage. Implemented by the home server and reached via pear service proxying.

post.description: Update a group's profile (name and/or description). Caller must be able to manage the group.

get.description: Get public info about this instance. Modeled on com.atproto.server.describeServer. No authentication required.

inviteRequired.description: Whether creating an org on this instance requires an invite token.

post.description: Notify another DID that there is an update for them on the fiven record.

recipient.description: The DID to grant permission to (URL parameter).

collection.description: The NSID of the record collection that the update is for.

rkey.description: The record key which was updated.

get.description: List apps connected to habitat for a given user. Returns connected apps for the authenticated user.

post.description: Add an admin to the org. Only callable by existing admins.

admin.description: The DID of the user to add as an admin.

post.description: Add member(s) to the org. Only callable by admins.
Create a new org with a bootstrap admin member.

admin_handle.description: Internal handle for the bootstrap admin (alphanumeric, 1-50 chars).

admin_password.description: Password for the bootstrap admin account (required for password login method).

handle_subdomain.description: Subdomain for all org member handles (e.g. 'acmecorp').

name.description: A display name for this org.

login_method.description: Login method for the org: 'password', 'atproto', or 'google'.

login_id.description: Provider-specific identifier (public ATProto DID for 'atproto', email for 'google'). Ignored for 'password'.

invite_token.description: Single-use invite token from an instance admin, required when the instance's org creation policy is invite_only.

org_id.description: The ID of the created org.

admin_did.description: The DID of the bootstrap admin.

admin_handle.description: The full handle of the bootstrap admin.

name.description: The display name of the created org.

post.description: Downgrade an admin to a regular member. Only callable by existing admins. The last admin cannot be downgraded.

admin.description: The DID of the admin to downgrade to member.

get.description: Get the list of admins in the org. Callable by any org member.
Get the list of members in the org. Callable by any org member.
Get general info about this organization.

parameters.name: orgId
parameters.description: The orge ID of the organization to look up. If not specified, defaults to the authenticated caller's org.

schema.description: The orge ID of the organization to look up. If not specified, defaults to the authenticated caller's org.

loginMethod.description: Login method for the org: 'password', 'atproto', or 'google'.

handleSubdomain.description: The subdomain used for all org member handles.

orgId.description: The unique ID of this organization.

post.description: Generate an invite token that can be sent to a member to join this organization.

expiresAt.description: When this token expires; defaults to 1 week.

reusable.description: Whether this token is reusable to invite more than one member; defaults to false.

post.description: Authenticate a habitat org member with their handle and password, returning a short-lived token for use in the OAuth callback flow.

handle.description: The full handle of the member (e.g. alice.example.com).

callbackURL.description: The URL to redirect the browser to in order to complete the OAuth flow.

post.description: Mint a new organization member identity with the given handle and token.

orgId.description: The ID of the org this member is joining.

handle.description: The internal handle (all letters + numbers, no special characters, does not include org domain) that will be used by the member.

token.description: The token that was issued by an org admin to allow members to join the organization.

password.description: The password for the new member's account (required for 'password' login method).

loginID.description: Provider-specific identifier (AT Protocol handle for 'atproto', email for 'google'). Required for non-password login methods.

handle.description: The full handle of the newly minted member identity.

did.description: The DID of the newly minted member identity.

post.description: Remove an admin from the org. Only callable by existing admins. The last admin cannot be removed.

admin.description: The DID of the admin to remove.

post.description: Remove member(s) from the org. Only callable by admins.
Grant read permission to a user or a clique for a specific collection or a specific record.

collection.description: The NSID of the lexicon or record to grant read permission for.

rkey.description: The Record Key to grant read permissions to, if any.

get.description: List read permissions visible to the authenticated user.

post.description: Revoke read permission from a user or a clique for a specific lexicon or record.

collection.description: The NSID of the lexicon or record to grant read permission for.

rkey.description: The Record Key to grant read permissions to, if any.

get.description: Check whether a subject holds a role on a space. The subject is either a user (DID) or a space-role userset (a space URI plus subjectRole), resolving through space-role usersets (groups, including org member/admin groups, are spaces, so group membership and nested groups resolve as space-role usersets) and built-in role implications (owner implies manager implies writer implies reader). Caller must have the reader role on the space.

parameters.name: subject
parameters.description: The subject to check: a user DID, or a space URI when checking a space-role userset. When a space URI, subjectRole is required.

schema.description: The subject to check: a user DID, or a space URI when checking a space-role userset. When a space URI, subjectRole is required.

parameters.name: subjectRole
parameters.description: The role held on the subject space, forming a userset. Required when subject is a space URI; omit when subject is a user DID.

schema.description: The role held on the subject space, forming a userset. Required when subject is a space URI; omit when subject is a user DID.

parameters.name: relation
parameters.description: The role to check for on the space.

schema.description: The role to check for on the space.

parameters.name: space
parameters.description: URI of the space.

schema.description: URI of the space.

allowed.description: Whether the subject holds the role on the space.

post.description: Delete a relationship tuple by its record URI. Caller must have the manager role on the tuple's governing space.

uri.description: URI of the tuple record to delete.

get.description: List the spaces on which a user holds a role, expanding space-role usersets (groups, including org member/admin groups, are spaces, so group membership and nested groups resolve as space-role usersets) and built-in role implications. Returns only spaces the caller has the reader role on.

parameters.name: did
parameters.description: DID of the user.

schema.description: DID of the user.

parameters.name: relation
parameters.description: The role to query for.

schema.description: The role to query for.

get.description: List the user DIDs that hold a role on a space, expanding space-role usersets (groups, including org member/admin groups, are spaces, so group membership and nested groups resolve as space-role usersets) and built-in role implications. Caller must have the reader role on the space.

parameters.name: space
parameters.description: URI of the space.

schema.description: URI of the space.

parameters.name: relation
parameters.description: The role to expand.

schema.description: The role to expand.

get.description: List relationship tuples governing a space, optionally filtered by object, subject, subject type, or relation. Caller must have the reader role on the space. This is the interoperable read surface other apps use to understand the permission structure.

parameters.name: space
parameters.description: URI of the governing space whose tuples to list.

schema.description: URI of the governing space whose tuples to list.

parameters.name: object
parameters.description: Optional. Restrict to tuples whose object is this space or group URI.

schema.description: Optional. Restrict to tuples whose object is this space or group URI.

parameters.name: subjectDid
parameters.description: Optional. Restrict to tuples whose subject is this user DID.

schema.description: Optional. Restrict to tuples whose subject is this user DID.

parameters.name: subjectType
parameters.description: Optional. Restrict to tuples whose subject is a user (userSubject) or a space userset (spaceRoleSubject).

schema.description: Optional. Restrict to tuples whose subject is a user (userSubject) or a space userset (spaceRoleSubject).

parameters.name: relation
parameters.description: Optional. Restrict to tuples with this relation.

schema.description: Optional. Restrict to tuples with this relation.

post.description: Write a relationship tuple, creating it if it does not already exist. The tuple record is owned by the org repo within its governing space. Caller must have the manager role on the object space.

relation.description: Role granted on the object space (owner|manager|writer|reader).

uri.description: URI of the written tuple record.

post.description: Create a pear repository record, creating it if it does not exist already, or updating it as needed.

repo.description: The handle or DID of the repo (aka, current account).

collection.description: The NSID of the record collection.

validate.description: Can be set to 'false' to skip Lexicon schema validation of record data, 'true' to require it, or leave unset to validate only for known Lexicons.

createGranteesClique.description: Whether to create a clique with the given grantees. If true, all grantees must be DIDs, and the created clique ref is returned.

uri.description: The habitat-uri of the put-ed object.

clique.description: If a clique was created, return its ref, formatted like clique:<owner did>/<clique key>

post.description: Delete a repository record, or ensure it doesn't exist. Requires auth, implemented by PDS.

repo.description: The handle or DID of the repo (aka, current account).

collection.description: The NSID of the record collection.

get.description: Get information about an account and repository, including the list of collections.
Get a blob associated with a given account. Returns the full blob as originally uploaded. Does not require auth; implemented by PDS.

parameters.name: did
parameters.description: The DID of the account.

schema.description: The DID of the account.

parameters.name: cid
parameters.description: The CID of the blob to fetch

schema.description: The CID of the blob to fetch

get.description: Get a single record from the pear repository.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The handle or DID of the repo.

schema.description: The handle or DID of the repo.

parameters.name: collection
parameters.description: The NSID of the record collection.

schema.description: The NSID of the record collection.

parameters.name: rkey
parameters.description: The Record Key.

schema.description: The Record Key.

parameters.name: includePermissions
parameters.description: Whether to return the permission grants on this record as part of the response.

schema.description: Whether to return the permission grants on this record as part of the response.

get.description: List records with optional filters for subjects, lexicons, and timestamps.

items.description: Repos (DIDs) to search from to retrieve records.

parameters.name: collection
parameters.description: Filter by specific lexicon.

schema.description: Filter by specific lexicon.

parameters.name: includePermissions
parameters.description: Whether to return the permission grants on this record as part of the response.

schema.description: Whether to return the permission grants on this record as part of the response.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: [UNIMPLEMENTED] Allow getting records that are strictly newer or updated since a certain time.

schema.description: [UNIMPLEMENTED] Allow getting records that are strictly newer or updated since a certain time.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: [UNIMPLEMENTED] The number of records to return. (Default value should be 50 to be consistent with atproto API).

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: [UNIMPLEMENTED] Cursor of the returned list.

schema.description: [UNIMPLEMENTED] Cursor of the returned list.

post.description: Write a pear repository record, creating it if it does not exist already, or updating it as needed.

repo.description: The handle or DID of the repo (aka, current account).

collection.description: The NSID of the record collection.

validate.description: Can be set to 'false' to skip Lexicon schema validation of record data, 'true' to require it, or leave unset to validate only for known Lexicons.

uri.description: The habitat-uri of the put-ed object.

post.description: Upload a new blob, to be referenced from a repository record. The blob will be deleted if it is not referenced within a time window (eg, minutes). Blob restrictions (mimetype, size, etc) are enforced when the reference is created. Requires auth, implemented by PDS.

get.description: Full-text search over records the caller's org has indexed.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: The search query text.

schema.description: The search query text.

post.description: Add a member to a space. Caller must have can_manage_members.

did.description: The DID of the user to add.

access.description: The access level to give the user. Defaults to read.

post.description: Create a new space. The authenticated user becomes the space owner. Requires auth, implemented by PDS.

type.description: The NSID of the space type, describing the modality of the space.

skey.description: The space key. Used to differentiate multiple spaces of the same type under the same owner. If not provided, one will be auto-generated.

post.description: Delete a record in a space, or ensure it doesn't exist. Caller must have can_delete on the space.

collection.description: The NSID of the record collection.

swapRecord.description: Compare and swap with the previous record by CID.

swapCommit.description: Compare and swap with the previous commit by CID.

post.description: Delete an entire space. Only the space owner can delete. All records in the space and all member relationships are removed.

space.description: URI of the space to delete.

get.description: Get the list of members in a space. Callable by any member of the space.

parameters.name: space
parameters.description: Reference to the space.

schema.description: Reference to the space.

get.description: Get a single record from a permissioned space. Callable by any space member.

parameters.name: space
parameters.description: Reference to the space.

schema.description: Reference to the space.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The DID of the member whose repo to read from. If omitted, defaults to the authenticated user.

schema.description: The DID of the member whose repo to read from. If omitted, defaults to the authenticated user.

parameters.name: collection
parameters.description: The NSID of the record collection.

schema.description: The NSID of the record collection.

parameters.name: rkey
parameters.description: The Record Key.

schema.description: The Record Key.

get.description: Get records modified since a given revision for a member in a space. Used for incremental sync. Callable by any member of the space.

parameters.name: space
parameters.description: Reference to the space.

schema.description: Reference to the space.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The DID of the member whose records to track.

schema.description: The DID of the member whose records to track.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: Return records with revisions after this value (exclusive).

schema.description: Return records with revisions after this value (exclusive).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of records to return.

cursor.description: The revision of the last returned record. Use as `since` in the next poll.

get.description: List records in a permissioned space, matching a specific collection. Callable by any member of the space.

parameters.name: space
parameters.description: Reference to the space.

schema.description: Reference to the space.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The DID of the member whose repo to read from. If omitted, defaults to the authenticated user.

schema.description: The DID of the member whose repo to read from. If omitted, defaults to the authenticated user.

parameters.name: collection
parameters.description: The NSID of the record type.

schema.description: The NSID of the record type.

parameters.name: reverse
parameters.description: Flag to reverse the order of the returned records.

schema.description: Flag to reverse the order of the returned records.

get.description: List the spaces that the authenticated user participates in, optionally filtered by type and/or owner DID. Requires auth.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Filter to spaces of this type.

schema.description: Filter to spaces of this type.

parameters.name: did
parameters.description: Filter to spaces owned by this DID.

schema.description: Filter to spaces owned by this DID.

post.description: Write a record in a permissioned space, creating or updating it as needed. Requires auth, implemented by PDS.

collection.description: The NSID of the record collection.

post.description: Remove a member from a space. Caller must have can_manage_members.

did.description: The DID of the user to remove.

network.habitat.clique.description: This collection is reserved for special purposes and cannot be directly written to. To read/set cliques, use clique-specific XRPC APIs.

network.habitat.collections.defs.collect.description: A record collection (lexicon type) present in the org's synced data, with a count of the records in it the calling user can see. A record scoped to more than one readable space is counted once per space, since each space holds its own version.

collection.description: The NSID of the record collection.

network.habitat.collections.defs.recordV.description: A single record scoped to one space. The same repo/collection/rkey in a different space is a distinct record with its own version, so it appears as its own recordView. The record body is not included; fetch it on demand from pear using the space, repo, collection and rkey.

uri.description: The space-record URI (spaceUri/repo/collection/rkey), unique to this record in this space.

space.description: URI of the space this record belongs to.

repo.description: DID of the repo the record lives in.

collection.description: The NSID of the record collection.

blob.description: Base64-encoded Yjs state update representing the document content.

docId.description: The doc's space key, used as the document identifier in updateDoc and routing.

title.description: The document title, from its markdown 'self' record.
The document title, derived from the first heading or line.

content.description: The rendered markdown content of the document.

network.habitat.grantee.clique.description: A clique grantee in the form clique:did:plc:web:arushi/clique-key

network.habitat.groups.defs.groupView.description: A group, backed by a network.habitat.group space, with its membership resolved. Membership is the set of users holding at least the writer role on the group-space, expanded through inherited groups.

isMember.description: Whether the calling user is a member of this group.

canManage.description: Whether the calling user can manage this group (add members, edit it).

direct.description: True if the member is granted a role directly on this group, false if the membership is inherited from another group.

viaGroup.description: If inherited, the URI of the group-space the membership came from.

lastUsed.description: The last time habitat detected a session with this app.

logoUri.description: The logo URI of this app.

grantee.description: The grantee of the permission — either a DID or a habitat clique URI.

collection.description: The NSID of the collection the permission applies to.

rkey.description: The record key the permission applies to. Empty string means the permission covers the entire collection.

effect.description: Whether this permission grants or denies access.

network.habitat.relationship.defs.spaceO.description: A space that a role is granted on.

network.habitat.relationship.defs.userSu.description: An individual user, identified by DID.

network.habitat.relationship.defs.spaceR.description: All subjects holding a role on a space (a userset). Enables cross-space inheritance, e.g. spaceA's writers as writers of spaceB.

relation.description: Role granted on the object space (owner|manager|writer|reader).

targetLexicon.description: The NSID of the lexicon this render schema applies to.

title.description: Human-readable name for this record type.

description.description: A brief description of what this record type represents.

network.habitat.render.schema.fieldSchem.description: Describes how to display a single field of a record.

path.description: Dot-notation path into the record value (e.g. 'name', 'startsAt').

optional.description: If true, omit this field from display when its value is missing or empty.

network.habitat.render.schema.datetime.description: Render as a formatted date/time string.

network.habitat.render.schema.badge.description: Render as a pill badge, extracting the token name from an NSID#token value.

network.habitat.render.schema.list.description: Render as a list of items.

network.habitat.render.schema.primary.description: Most prominent display — used for the record's main identifier (e.g. title).

network.habitat.render.schema.metadata.description: De-emphasized display, shown at the bottom or collapsed.

lastTouched.description: The last time a record in this collection was touched.

uri.description: URI reference to the record, formatted as a habitat-uri.

spaceUri.description: URI of the space the record belongs to.

recordType.description: The NSID of the record type.

snippet.description: A highlighted excerpt of the matching content.

type.description: The NSID of the space type.
